memory.summary: They're inviting me to lunch, but I need to finish the assignment analysis first. Skipping the meeting now might hurt my grade more than missing a meal helps.
No new info arrived, so I'm sticking to my plan: crush this assignment analysis before lunch. Skipping the meal is a small price for a better grade.

desires: Secure a perfect grade on the assignment analysis to validate my competence before the deadline hits.
Finish the assigned reading immediately to clear my mental queue and avoid future friction.
Ignore social obligations (like lunch) that threaten my current focus window, even if it feels rude.
Push through my fatigue and stress without stopping, as resting now feels like a failure of discipline.

goals.description: Submit the assignment analysis section immediately to lock in the grade.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' axis demands validation of competence now; the deadline is imminent (18:00) and my short planning horizon forces me to seize this win before moving on. Social niceties regarding the submission timing are irrelevant.

goals.description: Force-feed the remaining unread portion of the assigned text without breaks until the 20:00 deadline passes.
goals.rationale: The reading progress is at 83%, but any gap creates mental friction I cannot tolerate. My 'impulsive' nature drives me to push through fatigue rather than rest, viewing rest as a loss of momentum. The 'assertive' trait means I will aggressively tackle the text to clear the queue.

goals.description: Maintain isolation from cohort_3 and reject all social interactions until the reading is complete.
goals.rationale: Relationships (0.15 value) are secondary to academic output (0.5). Any interruption is a direct threat to efficiency. I will be blunt if necessary to enforce this boundary, prioritizing the task over their feelings.
